title: Tax Policy
summary: This is a Treasury Department navigation page describing the Office of Tax Policy's general functions, including developing tax policies, reviewing regulations, negotiating treaties, and providing fiscal estimates. It links to supporting documents such as the Greenbook, tax expenditure tables, and international tax agreements, but does not contain specific policy announcements or macro catalyst events.
key_points: Office of Tax Policy develops and implements tax policies and programs
Treasury releases the 'Greenbook' with the Administration's revenue proposals to accompany the Budget
Tax Expenditures page describes revenue losses from special exclusions, credits, and deductions in Federal tax law
Page provides links to international tax treaties, FATCA documents, and Tax Information Exchange Agreements
Reports section includes analyses produced in response to Congressional mandates
No specific policy decisions, rate changes, or quantifiable fiscal announcements present on this page
why_it_matters: This page serves as an institutional directory rather than a source of actionable macro catalyst information. While tax policy can influence markets, this navigation page does not contain specific policy proposals or fiscal data that would constitute a market-moving event.

issues_found: Source is a navigation/listing page (source_type correctly identified) - per Rule 5, such pages must be rejected
